Code: user-facing scheme-error string + a few comments + entitlements example (migration-history comments that say 'WDA replaced idb' kept). docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: current-state diagram/tables (ROADMAP migration log left intact). wiki/: Home front page bumped to v0.6.0 (MCP, agent-runs visibility, Sparkle) + idb prereqs dropped from Quick-Start/Contributing + HarnessCLI rationale.

author: Alan Wizemann
message:
Add Sparkle auto-update (appcast on GitHub Pages)

- Sparkle 2.x via SPM (app target only); SPUStandardUpdaterController + a 'Check for Updates…' menu command
- Partial Info.plist (SUFeedURL → GitHub Pages appcast, SUPublicEDKey) merged over Xcode's generated keys
- scripts/appcast.sh signs the release zip, runs generate_appcast, and publishes appcast.xml to gh-pages; wired into release.sh with a CFBundleVersion bump + appcast step after the GitHub release

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Alan Wizemann
message:
Add Agent Sessions GUI + thread agent runs into app History

Phase 3 of agent-runs visibility:
- Agent Sessions sidebar section + live-session monitor (polls marker files) + global session-running banner
- History origin badge (OriginBadge); agent rows title by goal, not the mcp-run sentinel
- MCP ad-hoc runs match-or-create an Application from their target, so they thread into per-app History instead of floating app-less; Agent Sessions rows navigate into that History
- Audit fixes: banner allowsHitTesting(false); stale-marker gate in AgentSessionStore.readAll; reload History on any ended session (by id); guard archived/missing app in openInHistory; propagate MCP app-list read error; LazyVStack + a11y combine

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Alan Wizemann
message:
Add RunOrigin + V6 migration + agent-session markers; show agent runs in History

RunOrigin (gui/mcp/cli) is stored on RunRecord.sourceRaw via a lightweight V6 SwiftData migration (full V5 freeze — the V1-V4 pattern; sharing the unchanged models triggered a duplicate-checksum crash). Value derives from the run name at skeleton time; RunRecordSnapshot.source falls back to name-derivation for legacy rows. Validated on the real store with data preserved.

AgentSessionMarker + AgentSessionStore: the MCP RunSupervisor writes a live per-run marker file (step/phase/goal) and clears it on finish, so the GUI can show agent activity cross-process. RunHistory no longer hides agent/CLI runs (no Application) under the app-scope filter. Safety: RunHistoryStore's reset-on-migration-failure path now archives the store to .corrupt-<timestamp> instead of deleting it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Alan Wizemann
message:
Add HarnessMCP server to drive Harness from agents; bound web-driver timeouts

HarnessMCP: a development-time stdio MCP server (hand-rolled JSON-RPC 2.0, zero new deps) that lets agents drive Harness — create personas/applications/actions/chains, stage credentials, start/cancel autonomous UI-testing runs, and read results + screenshots. New xcodegen 'tool' target built like HarnessCLI (shares Harness/ minus the SwiftUI surface); opens the GUI's on-disk SwiftData store so data is shared and seeds built-in personas. 16 tools, an idle watchdog, and terminal-record persistence on abnormal run end (no ghost rows). Registered in .mcp.json.

WebDriver: every per-step WKWebView await (settle/awaitDOMSettled, the Set-of-Mark probe, runJS/runJSAndReturn, captureSnapshot) is now bounded by a hard timeout via a new raceAgainstTimeout helper. Fixes web runs hanging indefinitely when a click navigates to a page that never finishes loading (the step budget couldn't break it). Benefits GUI runs too. Reviewed via a multi-agent fresh-eyes audit; applied hardening (JSON type-coercion guards, credential-stage ordering + cross-app ownership check, schema clarifications). Validated end-to-end against a live site.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Alan Wizemann
message:
docs(0.5): release notes + README/site updates for v0.5.0

- Release notes covering Local Mac inference (Ollama / native /api/chat),
  Set-of-Mark + smart-settle on iOS + macOS, shared MarkRenderer pipeline,
  HarnessCLI dev driver, WebContent log-flood fix, and supporting fixes
  (simctl exit-code tolerance, WDA timeout bump, non-persistent
  WKWebsiteDataStore, system-aware NSAppearance binding, per-turn
  behavior reminders).
- README "Unreleased" → "What's new in 0.5"; status line bumped to cover
  Set-of-Mark on all three platforms + Local Mac alongside cloud providers.
- Landing site: hero lede mentions cloud-or-fully-local; trust strip
  swaps "Vision-grounded" for "Cloud or fully-local models"; new FAQ
  entry for fully-local Harness; existing model + data-handling FAQs
  updated to reflect the dual track. llms.txt mirrors.
- Set-of-Mark on iOS + macOS marked shipped on the wiki Roadmap (in a
  separate wiki commit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Alan Wizemann
message:
fix(web): silence WebContent::markAllLayersVolatile log flood

GUI web runs were flooding Xcode's debug console (and the unified
log) with WebKit-internal messages:

  WebContent[…] - [sessionID=9223372036854775810]
    WebProcess::markAllLayersVolatile: Failed to mark layers as
    volatile for webPageID=…

Two compounding causes:

1. **Live-mirror poller cadence**. `RunCoordinator` polled
   `WebDriver.liveSnapshot()` every 333ms (3 fps) regardless of
   step latency. Cloud steps run 6-15s; local steps 60s+. The
   poller refreshes the live mirror UI in the GUI — useful — but
   3 fps is much faster than perception needs, and every tick
   touched layers WebKit wanted to volatilize. Cut to 1 fps:
   visible refresh is still smooth, log volume drops 3×.

2. **Off-screen WKWebView host window**. The window was placed at
   `(-10_000, -10_000)` to keep it out of the user's view without
   tripping AppKit's "you must be on a screen" warning. WebKit's
   layer-management policy treats that as effectively-invisible
   and aggressively schedules volatile-layer passes; each pass
   collides with our snapshot pipeline (the layers are needed
   for capture) and fires the "Failed to mark layers as volatile"
   log.

   Replaced with an on-screen-but-transparent placement: window
   at `(0, 0)` with `alphaValue = 0`, `ignoresMouseEvents = true`,
   window level set to `.normal - 1` so it sits behind every
   regular window, and `collectionBehavior` flagged
   `.canJoinAllSpaces / .stationary / .ignoresCycle` to keep it
   out of Mission Control / Cmd-Tab. From WebKit's perspective
   the view is now on a visible screen; the volatile-layer cycle
   doesn't fire. From the user's perspective the window is
   invisible.

Tests: 229/0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Alan Wizemann
message:
fix(ios): tolerate simctl screenshot exit-code flakes when the PNG is on disk

iOS CLI runs were intermittently failing at step 2 capture with
'Screenshot capture failed. Detected file type "PNG" from extension'.
Reproduction: tap_mark dispatch on step 1's WDA session works,
settle gate polls simctl io screenshot at 150ms cadence (~13 polls
over a 2s window), step 2's per-step capture call follows
immediately — and on Xcode 17 / iOS 26.2, simctl can exit non-zero
on rapid back-to-back invocations even though the PNG it just
wrote is intact on disk.

`simctl io booted screenshot <path>` ALWAYS prints "Detected file
type 'PNG' from extension" + "Wrote screenshot to: …" — the first
line is informational, not an error. Treating any non-zero exit
as fatal surfaced a confusing message and killed otherwise-healthy
runs.

`SimulatorDriver.screenshot` now:

  1. Runs simctl. Exit 0 → success.
  2. Exit non-zero BUT file exists on disk AND ≥256 bytes (PNG
     header + non-empty payload) → success. simctl wrote the
     valid PNG; the exit-code warning is benign.
  3. Otherwise retry once after 200ms.
  4. Two failures with no file → throw with the captured stderr.

256-byte threshold is well above the PNG signature (8 bytes) and
catches truly-empty writes; simctl never produces a partial PNG.

Validated on a Qwen3-VL 8B / Scarf Mobile run: step 1 succeeded,
prior runs failed at step 2 capture, the fixed driver got through
to step 7 with the full tap_mark → type sequence working.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
